programmes.show_description: UKIP and Mr Corbyn are the easy targets for satirical sharpshooters Dara O Briain, Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons, James Acaster, Rob Beckett, Ellie Taylor and Josh Widdicombe.
Clare Balding, Rhod Gilbert, Nick Grimshaw and Rob Delaney join regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack to sort the unbelievable facts from the plausible fiction. Rob Brydon hosts.
Rob Brydon hosts the devilishly fun panel show in which stars swap plausible porkies and far-fetched facts. With Richard Hammond, Sean Lock, Trevor Noah - and Judy Murray.
Rob Brydon hosts the compelling comedy panel show. Regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack tell cock and bull stories with guests Ken Livingstone, Reginald D Hunter and Stephen Mangan.
Frankie returns to help make sense of the bewildering times we live in through stand-up, reviews and discussion as he tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet.
Richard Ayoade and celebrity friends help improve Christmas with gadgets for shopping, wrapping presents, beating the weather, cooking the dinner and carol singing.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Weddings: Si and Dave explore how some of the foodie customs associated with the wedding ceremony have evolved and help prepare a Scottish wedding breakfast.
Island Life: The biker boys continue their countrywide hunt for great grub. They celebrate our love of fish and shellfish and find out about Billingsgate Market.
Christmas Special: The jolly panel show goes all Christmassy as Miranda Hart, Stephen Mangan, Barry Cryer and Miles Jupp join the three wise regulars.
Rob Brydon hosts the sly panel show as David Mitchell, Lee Mack, Fiona Bruce, Micky Flanagan, Steve Jones and Claudia Winkleman tell implausible truths and believable lies.
Bruno Tonioli, Adam Buxton, Kirsty Wark and Rob Beckett join brazen bluffers David Mitchell and Lee Mack to tell tall tales and uncomfortable truths. Rob Brydon presents.
Bob Mortimer and Mel Giedroyc are amongst the stars revealing improbable titbits about themselves, some of which are untrue. Can their fellow celebs pick out the porkies?
Lee Mack, David Mitchell, Miles Jupp, Heston Blumenthal, Emilia Fox and Ed Byrne try to separate tall tale from candid confession in the wily panel show. Rob Brydon hosts.
Does Kelly Hoppen wash with an orange? Did Rhod Gilbert once holiday in a French garage? Rob Brydon hosts the fact-or-fib panel show where celebs bluff and blab for points.
Comedy giant Greg Davies tells some tall tales as guest captain on the uproarious panel show where deceit is rewarded. With comic colossi Richard Osman and Phill Jupitus.
Star names turn 'true detective' in the mirthful fact-or-fiction panel show. Jo Brand, Roisin Conaty, Paul Foot and Ray Mears join Lee Mack, David Mitchell and Rob Brydon.
The regulars plus Ray Winstone, Josh Widdicombe, Rachel Riley and Ricky Tomlinson make Yule fools out of each other by mixing festive fibs with facts. Top panel show laughs.
Will David Mitchell, Lee Mack and guest panellists discern fact from fiction in the comedy panel show? Here, host Rob Brydon welcomes Marcus Brigstocke, Jamelia and Jimmy Carr.
Host Rob Brydon presides over crafty captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack in the compelling comedy panel show. With fab fibbers Miranda Hart, Clive Anderson and Claudia Winkleman.
Witty panel show all about truth and lies, hosted by Rob Brydon. Regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests Kelvin MacKenzie, Frankie Boyle and Jack Whitehall.
Host Rob Brydon joins regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the scheming panel show. Janet Street-Porter, Omid Djalili, Dave Gorman and Davina McCall are the guest fibbers.
Rob Brydon hosts the deceptive comedy panel show. Captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack trade porkies with guests Ronni Ancona, Chris Hoy, Gabby Logan and Danny Wallace.
Rob Brydon hosts the witty panel show about fanciful facts and plausible porkies. Joining David Mitchell and Lee Mack are Michael Ball, Charlie Brooker and Reece Shearsmith.
Famous fibbers Greg Rutherford, Kirsty Young, Joanna Scanlan and Henning Wehn deride their fellow guests' truth-handling abilities in the deceitful quiz hosted by Rob Brydon.
Panel show giggles. Jimmy Carr, Griff Rhys Jones, Dave Myers and Susanna Reid join Rob Brydon, David Mitchell and Lee Mack to discern unlikely fact from plausible fiction.
Compilation: Rob Brydon returns with a special episode of the rib-tickling, fib-tackling comedy panel show, featuring all the best outtakes and unseen bits from series 7.
Backwards: Off-the-wall space comedy with the huge fanbase. The hapless Kryten accidentally pilots the ship through a time hole and on to an alternate 20th century Earth.
Marooned: Stellar comedy. When the crew are forced to abandon ship, Lister and Rimmer find themselves stranded on a frozen moon with only a pot noodle and a tin of dog food.
Polymorph: Space escapades in the much-loved sitcom. A mutant is set loose aboard Red Dwarf and submits the crew to a hideous 24-hour nightmare, featuring killer underpants.
Enough: The gags come at a rat-a-tat-tat pace in Lee Mack's hit comedy. Lee and Lucy argue about having another child - then they witness what raising four kids would be like.

programmes.show_name: Not Going Out: The True Meaning of Christmas
programmes.show_description: Lee buys the perfect Christmas present for his children, but their grandparents have the same idea.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: An extended episode of the panel show. Guest host Jo Brand is joined by panellists Rylan Clark-Neal and journalist Robert Peston in taking a sideways look at the news.
The magnificent Lou Sanders introduces comedians Celya AB and Neil Delamere to the stage.
Maisie Adam introduces comedians Michael Odewale and Susie McCabe to the stage.
Rise and Shine: Richard Ayoade tests gadgets to get people get out of bed and out of the house in the morning with long-term early riser and DJ Sara Cox.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Food Pairings: Si and Dave explore how British ingredients can combine to make classic pairings. The duo create a pork and apple dish and a pear and stilton tart.
Newcastle: Not everything is black and white in Newcastle, as savvy bidders gamble on unseen storage units in the hit reality show. Feisty new girl Ione clashes with Shaun.
Northampton: Britain's answer to the riotous reality series. Ione goes head to head with Sam. And who will be left as a bridesmaid when a wedding carriage comes up for grabs?
Actor Steve Coogan joins the trio as they take a gander at the Aston Martin Vanquish, the Ferrari 575, the Nissan Skyline and cars from the Bond film Die Another Day.
Chat show king Jonathan Ross guests as Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe review the S Class Mercedes, the Audi A8, the Jaguar XJ and the Bentley Arnage T.
Simon Reeve travels through Texas and Mexico and joins the US Border Patrol - who pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the Rio Grande river in search of a new life - on the way.
In the fifth and final leg of his journey, Simon travels across Central America from the Caribbean to the Pacific by beginning in Belize and finishing his trip in Costa Rica.
Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the Rivers Esk and Tees with his dear friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse.
Pies: The hairy duo celebrate British food. They find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch and discover the story behind some of the nation's best loved pies.
Takeaways: The biker duo explore how Britain's love affair with takeaway food has evolved and cook some of their favourites in the Best of British kitchen.
Robson is joined by his uncle and nephew on a mission to try new activities in the great outdoors that include birdwatching and gliding.
Simon Reeve travels through Texas and Mexico and joins the US Border Patrol - who pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the Rio Grande river in search of a new life - on the way.
In the fifth and final leg of his journey, Simon travels across Central America from the Caribbean to the Pacific by beginning in Belize and finishing his trip in Costa Rica.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Safeguard: A nationwide investigation begins when Dorset Police uncover an international gang posing as couriers to scam vulnerable pensioners out of thousands of pounds.

programmes.show_name: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programmes.show_description: Operation Renaissance: Arson leads to a gunfight as Merseyside Police respond to two terrifying attacks on the Wirral and escalating violence results in a gang member being hunted.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: An extended episode of the panel show. Guest host Jo Brand is joined by panellists Rylan Clark-Neal and journalist Robert Peston in taking a sideways look at the news.
Reflections: Sandi Toksvig reflects on this and that in an episode themed around reflections with Alan Davies, Zoe Lyons, Joe Lycett and Liza Tarbuck.
George Osborne's weird Tory conference stance is meat and drink to savage panel show satirists Hugh Dennis, Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones, Ed Gamble and Holly Walsh.
